Name: Assets
Detail: Assets can be classified as Current assets or Non-current assets.

Name: Current assets
Detail:
A current asset is an asset (economic resource owned by the entity) that can be converted into cash, sold or otherwise used up within 12 months of period end. Current assets are often used in the normal course of business and constantly flow in and out of the entity.

Include

Any current assets not otherwise separately categorised.

Name: Cash and cash equivalents
Detail: Cash and cash equivalents can be classified as bank accounts/(overdraft) or cash floats

Name: Bank accounts/(overdraft)
Detail:
Include
  • Bank accounts in funds (cheque, commercial and savings accounts)
  • Bank accounts in overdraft
  • Foreign currency denominated accounts
  • Solicitors' trust accounts
  • Unpresented cheques and outstanding deposits

Notes: Include all highly liquid cash and bank investments that are used by the entity as part of its day-to-day cash management. This includes cheque and savings accounts (reconciled), cash, and demand deposits. Also include all bank accounts (reconciled) that are in overdraft. If there are unpresented cheques or outstanding deposits at period end, these should also be included here.

Exclude
  • Cash on hand (cash, petty cash, floats)
  • Credit cards
  • Term deposits

Notes: Do not include cash on hand (cash, petty cash, cash floats). Categorise these as Cash floats under Assets > Current Assets. Do not include credit cards. Categorise these as Trade and other payables under Liabilities > Current liabilities. Do not include term deposits. Categorise these as Investments (current) under Assets > Current assets, or Term deposits under Assets > Non-current assets > Investments.

Name: Cash floats
Detail:
Include
  • Cash on hand (cash, petty cash, floats)
Exclude
  • Bank accounts/(overdraft)
  • Credit cards
  • Term deposits

Notes: Do not include bank accounts/(overdraft). Categorise these as bank accounts/(overdraft) under Assets > Current Assets. Do not include credit cards. Categorise these as Trade and other payables under Liabilities > Current liabilities. Do not include term deposits. Categorise these as Investments (current) under Assets > Current assets, or Term deposits under Assets > Non-current assets > Investments.

Name: Loans to directors (current)
Detail:
Include
  • Director loans (current)
  • Director advances (current)

Notes: Include all advances/loans to directors or members of the governing body which are expected to be repaid within 12 months of period end.

Exclude
  • Director loans (non-current)
  • Shareholder loans

Name: Inventories
Detail:
Inventories include materials and finished products held by the entity for sale or service (such as merchandise, finished products and spare parts). Inventories can also be held to support production (such as raw materials) or to support activities (such as repairs, maintenance and consumables).

Include
  • Closing finished goods
  • Closing raw materials
  • Consumable stores and supplies

Notes: Include inventories (also known as stock) held by the entity at period end.

Exclude
  • Depreciable assets
  • Work in progress

Notes: Do not include Work in progress. Categorise this as Work in progress under Assets > Current assets > Inventories. Do not include capital assets as part of your inventories total.

Name: Work in progress
Detail:
Work in progress refers to materials and components that have begun their transformation to finished products, but remain unfinished at period end. In a business which provides services, work in progress may include contractual service agreements commenced but unfinished at period end. Work in progress is usually accounted for by way of a journal entry at period end.

Include
  • Closing work in progress
Exclude
  • Closing finished goods
  • Closing raw materials
  • Consumable stores and supplies

Name: Loan assets (current)
Detail: Include all advances/loans made, whether secured or unsecured, which are repayable within 12 months of period end.

Name: Trade and other receivables
Detail:
Essentially, a receivable is an amount owed to the entity.

Include
  • Other receivables (debtors)
  • Credit cards (if in funds)

Notes: Include any other receivables not otherwise separately categorised. Other receivables may include receivables arising from the sale of fixed assets, or credit cards (if in funds at period end).

Exclude
  • Trade receivables (trade debtors)

Name: Allowance for doubtful debts
Detail: An allowance for trade and other receivables due to the organisation within one year (or the normal operating cycle, whichever is longer) that are expected to be uncollectible.

Name: Prepayments
Detail:
A prepayment is an expense paid, either in full or part, before its due date. Common examples of prepayments are insurance and rent which are usually paid in advance.

Include
  • Prepaid insurance
  • Prepaid rent
  • Prepaid loan fees
Exclude
  • Provisional tax
  • Prepaid taxes

Name: Trade receivables
Detail:
Include
  • Trade receivables (debtors)
  • Allowance for doubtful debts

Notes: Include only trade receivables (also known as trade debtors). These represent the amounts owing to the entity at period end specifically from the sale of goods or services. Also include the allowance for bad or doubtful debts at period end. An allowance for bad or doubtful debts is essentially an estimation of uncollectible trade receivables at period end.

Exclude
  • Other receivables (debtors)
  • Trade receivables from related parties

Notes: Do not include other receivables (non trade receivables). Categorise these as Trade and other receivables under Assets > Current assets. Do not include trade receivables owed by related parties. Categorise these as Related party receivables/(payables) under Assets > Current assets.

Name: Related party receivables - current
Detail:
Parties are considered related if one party has the ability (directly or indirectly) to control or influence the other party in making operating, investing or financing decisions. Parties are also considered related when they are subject to common outside control or significant influence. Related parties may include subsidiaries, joint ventures, partnerships and associates.

Include
  • Related party receivables (current)
  • Advances to related parties (current)
  • Loans to related parties (current)
  • Trade receivables from related parties

Notes: Include all advances/loans to or from related parties which are expected to be paid within 12 months of period end.

Exclude
  • Advances from related parties (current)
  • Advances from related parties (non-current)
  • Advances to related parties (non-current)
  • Beneficiary advance accounts
  • Loans from related parties (current)
  • Loans from related parties (non-current)
  • Loans to related parties (non-current)
  • Partner advance accounts
  • Proprietor advance accounts
  • Related party payables (current)
  • Related party payables (non-current)
  • Related party receivables (non-current)
  • Settlor loans (if entity is a trust)
  • Shareholder advance accounts
  • Sole trader advance accounts

Notes: Do not include shareholder, partner, sole trader or beneficiary advance accounts. Categorise these as Proprietor/shareholder advance accounts under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities.

Name: Term deposits (current)
Detail: Amount of term deposits due to mature within 12 months of period end.

Name: User defined current asset
Detail: User defined current asset report codes are used to display a particular current asset or group of current assets that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined current asset 1
Detail: User defined current asset report codes are used to display a particular current asset or group of current assets that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined current asset 2
Detail: User defined current asset report codes are used to display a particular current asset or group of current assets that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined current asset 3
Detail: User defined current asset report codes are used to display a particular current asset or group of current assets that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: Non current assets
Detail:
Non-current assets are assets (economic resources owned by the entity) that cannot easily be converted into cash. They are usually bought with the intention of being used for an extended period of time. In addition, they are not usually directly sold to customers.

Include

Include any non-current assets not otherwise separately categorised.

Name: Loans to directors (non-current)
Detail:
Include
  • Director loans (non-current)
  • Director advances (non-current)

Notes: Include all advances/loans to directors or members of the governing body which are expected to be repaid in a period extending beyond 12 months of period end.

Exclude
  • Director loans (current)

Name: Fixed assets
Detail:
A fixed asset is considered owned when the entity has legal title to that asset.

Include
  • Any fixed assets not otherwise separately categorised.
Exclude
  • Do not include fixed assets that are leased by the entity. Categorise these as Leased fixed assets under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets.

Name: Buildings at cost
Detail:
Buildings include leasehold improvements and alterations, marina berths and hangars.

Include
  • Buildings at cost
  • Hangars at cost
  • Marina berth at cost
  • Alterations at cost
  • Leasehold improvements at cost
  • Capitalised development expenditure at cost

Notes: Include the total cost of buildings. Also include any cost attributable to bringing and installing the asset(s) in its needed location, such as freight, installation costs and import duties.

Exclude
  • Accumulated depreciation of buildings
  • Buildings revaluation differential

Notes: If buildings have been revalued, do not include the revaluation differential. Categorise this as Buildings revaluation under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets. Do not include the accumulated depreciation of buildings. Categorise these as Accumulated depreciation of buildings under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets > Buildings at cost.

Name: Accumulated depreciation of buildings
Detail:
Include
  • Accumulated depreciation of buildings

Notes: Include the accumulated depreciation of buildings. This represents the total depreciation that has taken place up to period end on buildings.

Name: Impairment of fixed assets
Detail:
Include
  • Impairment of fixed assets

Notes: Include the Impairment of fixed assets. This represents the total impairment that has taken place up to period end on fixed assets.

Name: Land at cost
Detail:
Include
  • Total cost of land
Exclude
  • Land revaluation differential

Notes: If land has been revalued, do not include the revaluation differential. Categorise this as Land revaluation under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets.

Name: Land revaluation
Detail:
Include
  • Land revaluation differential

Notes: If land has been revalued, include the amount by which land has been revalued above historical cost - that is, the revaluation differential only.

Exclude
  • Land at cost

Notes: Do not include the original cost of land. Categorise this as Land at cost under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets.

Name: Leasehold improvements at cost
Detail:
Include
  • Leasehold improvements at cost
Exclude
  • Accumulated amortisation of leasehold improvements

Name: Accumulated amortisation of leasehold improvements
Detail:
Include
  • Accumulated amortisation of leasehold improvements
Exclude
  • Leasehold improvements at cost

Name: Plant and equipment at cost
Detail:
Include
  • Loose tools owned (pooled cost greater than $500)
  • Equipment owned
  • Plant owned

Notes: Include the total amount of plant and equipment that are owned by the entity. Also include any cost attributable to bringing and installing the asset(s) in its needed location, such as freight, installation costs and import duties.

Exclude
  • Accumulated depreciation of plant and equipment

Notes: Do not include the accumulated depreciation of plant and equipment. Categorise this as Accumulated depreciation of plant and equipment under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets > Plant and equipment at cost.

Name: Accumulated depreciation of plant and equipment
Detail:
Include
  • Accumulated depreciation of plant and equipment

Notes: Include the accumulated depreciation of plant and equipment that are owned by the entity. This represents the total depreciation that has taken place up to period end on owned plant and equipment.

Name: Buildings revaluation
Detail:
Buildings include leasehold improvements and alterations, marina berths and hangars.

Include
  • Buildings revaluation differential
  • Hangars revaluation differential
  • Marina berth revaluation differential

Notes: If buildings have been revalued, include the amount by which buildings have been revalued above historical cost - that is, the revaluation differential only.

Exclude
  • Buildings at cost
  • Accumulated depreciation of buildings

Notes: Do not include the original cost of buildings. Categorise these as Buildings at cost under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets. Do not include the accumulated depreciation of buildings. Categorise these as Accumulated depreciation of buildings under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets > Buildings at cost.

Name: Vehicles at cost
Detail:
A vehicle relates to any conveyance that transports people or objects, and can include cars, trucks, boats, trailers, motorbikes, tractors, caravans and aircraft.

Include
  • Aircraft owned
  • Bicycles owned
  • Boats owned
  • Caravans owned
  • Cars owned (motor vehicles)
  • Farm quad owned
  • Forklift owned
  • Helicopters owned
  • Motorcycles owned
  • Tractors owned
  • Trailers owned
  • Trucks owned

Notes: Include the total amount of vehicles that are owned by the entity. Also include any cost attributable to bringing and installing the asset(s) in its needed location, such as freight, installation costs and import duties.

Exclude
  • Accumulated depreciation of vehicles
  • Vehicle repairs and maintenance

Notes: Do not include the accumulated depreciation of vehicles owned. Categorise this as Accumulated depreciation of vehicles under Assets > Non-current assets > Fixed assets > Vehicles at cost.

Name: Accumulated depreciation of vehicles
Detail:
Include
  • Accumulated depreciation of vehicles owned

Notes: Include the accumulated depreciation of vehicles that are owned by the entity. This represents the total depreciation that has taken place up to period end on owned vehicles.

Name: Intangibles
Detail:
Intangible assets are non physical in form yet they provide benefits (future service potential) to the entity or give some kind of advantage in the marketplace.

Include
  • Franchise establishment fees
  • Customer lists
  • Branding
  • Websites

Notes: Include all intangible assets not otherwise separately categorised. Also include the accumulated amortisation of intangible assets not otherwise separately categorised.

Name: Accumulated amortisation of intangibles
Detail:
Include
  • Accumulated amortisation of intangibles

Notes: Include the accumulated amortisation of intangibles. This represents the total amortisation that has taken place up to period end on intangibles.

Name: Distribution rights
Detail:
Distribution rights represent the exclusive right of a copyright owner to distribute copies of the original work (book, illustration, photograph, record, software, etc.) to the public by sale, lease, or rental.

Include
  • Distribution rights

Notes: Include all distribution rights owned by the entity.

Exclude
  • Distribution rights amortisation
  • Distribution rights impairment

Notes: Do not include distribution rights amortisation or impairment. Categorise this as Distribution rights amortisation or Distribution rights impairment under Assets > Non-current assets > Intangibles > Distribution rights.

Name: Distribution rights amortisation
Detail:
Include
  • Distribution rights amortisation

Notes: Include the accumulated amortisation of distribution rights. This represents the total amortisation that has taken place up to period end on distribution rights.

Name: Distribution rights impairment
Detail:
Include
  • Distribution rights impairment

Notes: Include the accumulated impairment of distribution rights. This represents the total impairment that has taken place up to period end on distribution rights.

Name: Goodwill
Detail:
Goodwill is the amount by which the purchase price for a business combination exceeds the value of the identifiable assets, liabilities and contingent liabilities at the date of acquisition. Goodwill usually represents the reputation and customer base that the acquired business has developed whilst in operation.

Include
  • Goodwill
Exclude
  • Goodwill impairment

Notes: Do not include goodwill impairment. Categorise this as Goodwill impairment under Assets > Non-current assets > Intangibles > Goodwill.

Name: Goodwill impairment
Detail:
Include
  • Goodwill impairment

Notes: Include the accumulated impairment of goodwill. This represents the total impairment that has taken place up to period end on goodwill.

Name: Impairment of intangibles
Detail:
Include
  • Impairment of intangibles

Notes: Include the accumulated impairment of intangibles. This represents the total impairment that has taken place up to period end on intangibles.

Name: Financial assets
Detail:
Include

Include other investments not otherwise separately categorised, and that are expected to be held for a period extending beyond 12 months of period end. Investments are assets held by the entity for the accretion of wealth by way of revenues - such as interest, royalties, dividends, rentals and capital appreciation.

Exclude
  • Investments in subsidiaries
  • Investments in associates
  • Investments in joint ventures
  • Investments in partnerships
  • Investment properties

Name: Preference shares
Detail:
Preference shares differ to ordinary shares in that they generally do not carry voting rights and they attract regular and fixed dividends (whereas dividends on ordinary shares are uncertain and variable). If dividends on preference shares are not paid, they usually accrue until they can be paid. In the event of winding up, preference shares are usually repayable at par value, and rank above the claims of ordinary shareholders.

Include
  • Preference shares

Notes: Include all preference share investments that are expected to be held for a period extending beyond 12 months of period end.

Name: Shares and debentures
Detail:
Include
  • Ordinary shares
  • Shares
  • Accumulated capital
  • Debentures

Notes: Include all ordinary share and debenture investments that are expected to be held for a period extending beyond 12 months of period end. These investments are generally held by the entity for accretion of wealth by way of dividends and interest revenues.

Name: Term deposits
Detail:
A term deposit is a monetary deposit at a financial institution that cannot be withdrawn for a certain period of time (term). When the term is over, the initial investment can be withdrawn or reinvested. Generally, the longer the term the better the interest yield.

Include
  • Term deposits
  • Bonds
  • Commercial bills
  • Long term deposits

Notes: Include all interest bearing term deposits that are expected to be held for a period extending beyond 12 months of period end.

Exclude
  • Term deposits (current)

Notes: Do not include term deposits that are expected to be realised within 12 months of period end. Categorise these as Investments (current) under Assets > Current assets.

Name: Loan assets (non-current)
Detail: Include all advances/loans made, whether secured or unsecured, which are repayable over a period extending beyond 12 months after period end.

Name: Research and development
Detail:
Research and development are costs incurred to discover new knowledge about products, processes, and services, in order to create new and improved products, processes, and services that meet market needs.

Include
  • Development costs
  • Scientific development
  • Scientific research

Notes: Include the total amount incurred for research and development. Examples include farm development, drug development, machinery research and other scientific research and development.

Name: Impairment of research and development
Detail:
Include
  • Impairment of research and development

Notes: Include the accumulated impairment of research and development. This represents the total impairment that has taken place up to period end on research and development.

Name: Related party receivables - non current
Detail:
Parties are considered related if one party has the ability (directly or indirectly) to control or influence the other party in making operating, investing or financing decisions. Parties are also considered related when they are subject to common outside control or significant influence. Related parties include subsidiaries, joint ventures, partnerships and associates.

Include
  • Related party receivables (non-current)
  • Advances to related parties (non-current)
  • Loans to related parties (non-current)

Notes: Include all advances/loans to or from related parties which are expected to be paid in a period extending beyond 12 months of period end.

Exclude
  • Advances from related parties (current)
  • Advances from related parties (non-current)
  • Advances to related parties (current)
  • Beneficiary advance accounts
  • Loans from related parties (current)
  • Loans from related parties (non-current)
  • Loans to related parties (current)
  • Partner advance accounts
  • Proprietor advance accounts
  • Related party payables (current)
  • Related party payables (non-current)
  • Related party receivables (current)
  • Settlor loans (if entity is a trust)
  • Shareholder advance accounts
  • Sole trader advance accounts

Notes: Do not include shareholder, partner, sole trader or beneficiary advance accounts. Categorise these as Proprietor/shareholder advance accounts under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities. If the entity is a trust, do not include settlor advances. Categorise these as Unsecured loans under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities > Loans.

Name: User defined non current asset

Detail:

Exclude

  • Beneficiary drawings

Name: Gifting
Detail:
Any gifting received during the financial year from settlors as forgiveness of debt.

Include
  • Gifting

Detail:
Include
  • Profit distributions to partners
Exclude
  • Profit distributions to beneficiaries
  • Profit distributions to shareholders

Notes: Do not include beneficiary and shareholder profit distributions. Categorise these as beneficiary/shareholder profit distribution under Liabilities > Non current liabilities.

Detail:
Include
  • Profit distributions to partners
Exclude
  • Profit distributions to beneficiaries
  • Profit distributions to shareholders

Notes: Do not include beneficiary and shareholder profit distributions. Categorise these as beneficiary/shareholder profit distribution under Liabilities > Non current liabilities.

Detail:

  • Profit distributions to partners
Exclude
  • Profit distributions to beneficiaries
  • Profit distributions to shareholders

Notes: Do not include beneficiary and shareholder profit distributions. Categorise these as beneficiary/shareholder profit distribution under Liabilities > Non current liabilities.

Detail:

Name: Reserves
Detail:
Include
  • General reserves

Notes: Include all other reserves not otherwise separately categorised. These may include general reserves where the entity sets aside part of its retained earnings for a specified purpose (and hence the funds are unavailable for distribution).

Name: Foreign currency translation reserve
Detail:
These are unrealised gains or losses resulting from the translation of financial reports at period end that are denominated in a currency other than the reporting currency. For example, this would arise when translating a branch or subsidiary's financial statements into the reporting currency. This is quite different from the gains or losses resulting from the conversion of transactional items which are accounted for in the income statement.

Include
  • Foreign currency translation reserve
Exclude
  • Realised foreign currency gains or losses
  • Unrealised foreign currency gains or losses

Name: Livestock revaluation reserve
Detail:
Include
  • Herd livestock revaluation

Name: Revaluation reserves
Detail:
A revaluation reserve records revaluations (unrealised gains) of non-current assets. Generally, this amount represents the difference between the market value of assets and their original cost.

Include
  • Revaluation reserve
  • Financial assets reserve

Name: Retained earnings/Accumulated funds
Detail:
Retained earnings is the sum of all surpluses retained by the entity since its inception. Retained earnings is reduced by any losses incurred during a period of activity.

Include
  • Retained earnings brought forward

Notes: Include retained earnings (also known as accumulated earnings/losses) brought forward.

Name: Current year earnings
Detail:
Include
  • Current year surplus
  • Current year loss
  • Include the entity's current period earnings/losses.

Name: Dividends paid reserve
Detail: Dividends paid can be classified as Dividends paid on ordinary shares or Dividends paid on preference shares.

Name: Dividends paid on ordinary shares
Detail:
Include
  • Dividends paid on ordinary shares

Notes: Include all dividends declared on the entity's ordinary shares during the period.

Name: Dividends paid on preference shares
Detail:
Include
  • Dividends paid on preference shares

Notes: Include all dividends accumulated on the entity's preference shares during the period.

Name: Settlement sum
Detail:
Include
  • Include all settlement sums received from settlors.

Name: Share capital
Detail:
Include
  • Paid up share capital

Notes: Include all of the entity's paid up share capital. This generally represents the nominal value of a company's shares multiplied by the number of shares on issue, plus share premiums if any.

Name: Ordinary shares
Detail:
Include
  • Ordinary share capital

Notes: Include all of the entity's ordinary shares on issue.

Name: Share buy-back (at nominal value)
Detail:
Include
  • Share buy-back (at nominal value)

Notes: Include all of the entity's share buy-back (at nominal value).

Name: Convertible redeemable preference shares
Detail:
Include
  • Convertible redeemable preference shares

Notes: Include all of the entity's convertible redeemable preference shares.

  • Magazines and periodicals

  • Postage and stationery

  • Uniforms

Notes: Include any expenses which have not otherwise been categorised.

Name: Administrative expenses
Detail: General administration expenses associated with the general running of the business. For operating expenses associated with producing goods or services, use Expenses > Operating expenses.

Name: Advertising & marketing
Detail: Notes: Include sales publications,trade fairs, exhibitions, client training, market research, PR expenses, advertising and promotional costs.

Name: Amortisation of intangibles expense
Detail:
Include
  • Amortisation of intangibles expense

Notes: Include the current period charge for amortisation (write-off) of intangibles.

Name: Audit fees
Detail:
Include
  • Audit fees

Notes: Include all fees incurred in relation to auditors who are charged with providing the audit report on the financial report of the entity.

Exclude

Include

Exclude

  • Provision for doubtful debts
  • Debt collection fees

Notes: Do not include any provision for bad or doubtful debts. Categorise this as Provision for doubtful debts under Expenses > Bad debts.

Name: Provision for doubtful debts
Detail:
Include
  • Provision for bad or doubtful debts

Notes: Include the provision (or change in provision) for bad or doubtful debts at period end.

Name: Losses applied from prior periods
Detail: Accumulated losses applied to the profit of the financial year.

Name: Opening WIP
Detail:
The opening work in progress is the same as closing work in progress from the previous period end.

Include
  • Opening work in progress

Notes: Include the opening work in progress at the start of the period.

Name: Depreciation
Detail:
Depreciation is an expense that reflects the decline in service potential of fixed assets.

Include
  • Depreciation

Notes: Include the total current period depreciation charge for assets.

Exclude
  • Loss on sale of fixed assets
  • Gain on sale of fixed assets
  • Depreciation recovered on sale of fixed assets
  • Amortisation of intangibles

Notes: Do not include loss on sale, depreciation recovered on fixed assets or amortisation of intangibles. Categorise these respectively as Loss on disposal of property, plant and equipment under Expenses, Gain on disposal of property, plant and equipment under Revenue > Other revenue, or Amortisation of intangibles under Expenses.

Name: Distribution to beneficiary/partner
Detail:
Any distributions of income made during the financial year to beneficiaries or partners.

Include
  • Beneficiary/Partner distributions

Name: Dividends paid expense
Detail:
Include
  • Dividends paid on ordinary shares
  • Dividends paid on preference shares

Notes: when paying dividends from the income statement rather than from equity.

Name: Employment Costs
Detail: Employment Costs can be classified as Shareholder salaries, Superannuation expenses or Wages and salaries.

Name: Shareholder salaries
Detail:
Include
  • Shareholders' salaries
Exclude
  • Bonuses
  • Executive remuneration
  • Managers' salaries
  • PAYGW
  • Redundancy payments
  • Remuneration
  • Directors' fees
  • Sick pay
  • Holiday pay
  • Wages
  • Direct wages

Notes: Do not include any wages or salaries that have had PAYG deducted. Categorise these as Wages and salaries under Expenses. Do not include payments to directors or members of the governing body. Categorise these as Directors/trustee fees under Expenses.

Name: Superannuation expenses
Detail:
Include
  • Superannuation expenses

Name: Wages and salaries
Detail:
Include
  • Bonuses
  • Executive remuneration
  • Managers' salaries
  • PAYGW
  • Redundancy payments
  • Remuneration
  • Sick pay
  • Holiday pay
  • Wages
  • Direct wages

Notes: Include all costs incurred in relation to salaries, wages, executive remuneration, bonuses,managers' salaries, PAYG, sick pay and holiday pay.

Exclude
  • Directors' fees
  • Director drawings
  • Shareholder drawings
  • Beneficiary drawings
  • Partner drawings
  • Proprietor drawings
  • Penalties
  • Interest charged

Notes: Do not include payments to subcontractors. Categorise these as Contractor payments under Expenses. Do not include payments to directors or members of the governing body. Categorise these as Directors/trustee fees under Expenses.

Name: Entertainment
Detail:
Include all entertainment expenditure incurred which is allowed as a tax deduction.

Include
  • Deductible entertainment

Name: Non deductible entertainment
Detail:
Include all entertainment expenditure which is not allowed as a tax deduction.

Include
  • Non-deductible entertainment adjustment

Name: Non recurring items
Detail:
Include all non-recurring items. Non-recurring items are those items of revenue or expense which derive from events or transactions that are not expected to occur frequently, are distinct from the ordinary operations of the entity, and are outside the control or influence of managers or owners.

Include
  • Non-recurring items

Detail:
Include

Detail:

  • Pasture maintenance and improvement expenses

Detail:
Include
  • Fringe benefit tax

Notes: Include the actual fringe benefit tax paid during the period.

Exclude
  • Penalties
  • Interest charged

Notes: Do not include any penalties and interest charged. Categorise these respectively under Other non-deductible items and Interest and finance charges under Expenses.

Name: Foreign currency gains/losses
Detail: Foreign currency gains and losses can be classified as Realised foreign currency gains and losses or Unrealised foreign currency gains and losses.

Name: Realised foreign currency gains and losses
Detail:
Include realised foreign currency gains or losses. These usually arise on settlement of monetary items at rates different from those at which they were recognised during the period.

Include
  • Realised foreign currency gains
  • Realised foreign currency losses
Exclude
  • Unrealised foreign currency gains
  • Unrealised foreign currency losses

Notes: Do not include unrealised foreign currency gains or losses. Categorise these as Unrealised gains and losses under Expenses > Foreign currency gains and losses. Do not include gains or losses on translation. Categorise these as Foreign currency translation reserve under Equity > Reserves.

Name: Unrealised foreign currency gains and losses
Detail:
Include
  • Unrealised foreign currency gains
  • Unrealised foreign currency losses

Notes: Include unrealised foreign currency gains or losses. These usually arise on reporting of monetary items at rates different from those at which they were recognised during the period.

Exclude
  • Realised foreign currency gains
  • Realised foreign currency losses

Notes: Do not include realised foreign currency gains or losses. Categorise these as Realised gains and losses under Expenses > Foreign currency gains and losses. Do not include gains or losses on translation. Categorise these as Foreign currency translation reserve under Equity > Reserves.

Name: Impairment loss
Detail:
An impairment loss arises when the carrying amount of an asset exceeds its recoverable amount. If appropriate, the recoverable amount can be established by discounting future net cash flows expected to be received from that asset's use. An impairment loss reduces an overstated carrying amount (book value) to fair value.

Include
  • Impairment losses

Name: Income tax expense
Detail:
Income tax expense represents the tax payable on taxable income calculated using applicable tax rates.

Include
  • Current period income tax expense
  • Deferred tax expense

Notes: Include the current period income tax expense and deferred tax expense.

Exclude
  • Withholding tax paid

Name: Insurance expenses
Detail:
Include
  • Insurance
  • Medical insurance
  • Staff medical insurance
Exclude
  • Vehicle insurance

Notes: Do not include vehicle insurance. Categorise this as Vehicle expenses under Expenses.

Name: Interest and finance charges
Detail:
Include
  • Loan interest
  • Shareholders' interest
  • Other interest charged

Notes: Include interest and other costs incurred by the entity in connection with borrowing of funds or the costs associated with finance leases (hire purchase leases). Include interest paid to shareholders.

Exclude
  • Bank fees

Name: Investment expenses
Detail: Expenses relating to holding an investment.

Name: Rental investment expenses
Detail: Expenses relating to holding rental property investments.

Name: Loss on disposal of property, plant and equipment
Detail:
A loss will arise when the sale price of an asset is lower than that asset's carrying amount.

Include
  • Assets written off
  • Loss on disposal
  • Loss on scrapping

Notes: Include the total losses incurred on disposal, scrapping or writing off of fixed assets.

Exclude
  • Gain on sale of fixed assets

Notes: Do not include depreciation recovered or capital gains. Categorise these respectively as Gain on sale of property, plant and equipment under Revenue > Other revenue and Capital gains on sale of assets under Revenue > Other revenue > Gain on disposal of property, plant and equipment.

Name: Other non deductible items
Detail:
Include
  • Penalties

Notes: Include other expenses which are not allowed as a tax deduction.

Exclude

    Notes: Do not include non-deductible entertainment. Categorise these as Non-deductible entertainment under Expenses > Entertainment.

Name: Occupancy expense
Detail:
Expenses related to running costs of the organisation's premises.

Include
  • Rates
  • Service charges
  • Cleaning
  • Security
  • Use of home
Exclude
  • Rent (use Expenses > Rental and lease payments)
  • Utilities (use Expenses > Utilities)
  • Insurance (use Expenses > Insurance)
  • Repairs (use Expenses > Repairs & maintenance)

Name: Operating expenses
Detail: Expenses incurred in activities associated with production of goods or services. For administrative expenses not associated with producing goods or services, use Expenses > Administrative expenses.

Name: Professional fees
Detail:
Include all professional and accounting fees.

Include
  • Accountancy
  • Legal expenses
  • Other professional and legal fees
Exclude
  • Audit fees. Categorise these as Expenses > Audit fees

Name: Rental and lease payments
Detail:
Essentially, an operating lease is a rental agreement which grants the lessee (entity/you) use of an asset, whilst the lessor retains ownership rights, risks and rewards of that asset. Another feature of operating leases is that their lease term is shorter than the asset's useful life.

Include
  • Premises rental
  • Ground rent
  • Operating leases
  • Vehicle leases
  • Short term asset hire
  • Fishing quotas paid
  • Forklift hire

Notes: Include all costs of leasing and renting items which if purchased would be fixed assets. This includes premises rental and operating lease payments.

Exclude
  • Finance leases
  • Hire purchase leases

Name: Operating lease payments
Detail:
Include
  • Operating leases

Notes: Include operating lease payments which are required to be disclosed separately to rental lease payments.

Name: Repairs & maintenance
Detail:
Include
  • Repairs and maintenance on premises
  • Plant and machinery renewal, repairs and maintenance
  • Vehicle repairs, renewals and maintenance
  • General maintenance

Name: Travel and accomodation
Detail: Travel and accommodation can be classified as International travel and accommodation or National travel and accommodation.

Name: International travel and accomodation
Detail:
Include
  • International accommodation
  • International travel
  • International taxi fares
  • Airport parking fees

Name: National travel and accommodation
Detail:
Include
  • National accommodation
  • National travel
  • National taxi fares
  • Airport parking fees

Notes: Include all national (local/domestic) travel and accommodation costs.

Name: User defined expense
Detail: User defined expense report codes are used to display a particular expense or group of expenses that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined expense 1
Detail: User defined expense report codes are used to display a particular expense or group of expenses that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined expense 2
Detail: User defined expense report codes are used to display a particular expense or group of expenses that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined expense 3
Detail: User defined expense report codes are used to display a particular expense or group of expenses that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined expense 4
Detail: User defined expense report codes are used to display a particular expense or group of expenses that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: User defined expense 5
Detail: User defined expense report codes are used to display a particular expense or group of expenses that do not have an existing report code available.

Name: Utilities
Detail:
Include all utility costs.

Include
  • Electricity
  • Gas
  • Water
  • Other utilities

Name: Vehicle expenses
Detail:
A vehicle relates to any conveyance that transports people or objects, and can include cars, trucks, boats, trailers, motorbikes, tractors, caravans and aircraft.

Include
  • Berthage
  • Fuel
  • Road user charges
  • Warrant of fitness
  • Vehicle insurance
  • Vehicle maintenance

Notes: Include all expenditure incurred on vehicle running and maintenance. Common vehicle expenses include road user charges, insurance, registrations and maintenance.

Exclude
  • Vehicle purchases
  • Vehicle leasing
  • Fringe benefit tax

Notes: Do not include vehicle renting/leasing. Categorise this as Rental and lease payments under Expenses. Do not include Fringe Benefit Tax paid. Categorise this as Fringe benefit tax under Expenses.

Name: Liabilities
Detail: Liabilities can be classified as current liabilities or non-current liabilities.

Name: Current Liabilities
Detail:
Current liabilities are obligations that arise in the normal course of a business and are due for payment within 12 months of period end.

Include

Include any current liabilities not otherwise separately categorised.

Name: Chattel mortgage (Current)
Detail:
Include
  • Obligations under chattel mortgages
Exclude
  • Hire purchase obligations (use Liabilities > Current Liabilities > Hire Purchase (Current))
  • Other creditors
  • Trade creditors
  • Amounts owed to group undertakings

Notes: Include all amounts which are expected to be paid within 12 months of period end. If the amounts are expected over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end, include only the portion that is due within one year.

Name: Chattel mortgage: Unexpired Interest (Current)
Detail:
Include
  • Unexpired interest on chattel mortgages

Notes: Include all unexpired interest due within 12 months of period end. If the amounts are due over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end, include only the portion that is due within one year.

Name: Deferred income
Detail:
Deferred income is income the entity has received prior to selling a product or providing a service.

Include
  • Deferred income
Exclude
  • Deferred government grants income
  • Deferred lease incentives

Notes: Do not include Deferred government grants income. Categorise these as deferred government grants income under Liabilities > Current liabilities > Deferred income. Do not include Deferred lease incentives. Categorise these as deferred lease incentives under Liabilities > Current liabilities > Deferred income.

Name: Deferred government grants income
Detail:
Include
  • Deferred government grants income

Name: Deferred lease incentives
Detail:
Include
  • Deferred lease incentives

Name: Hire purchase (current)
Detail:
Include
  • Obligations under hire purchase agreements
Exclude
  • Chattel mortgages (use Liabilities > Current Liabilities > Chattel Mortgage (Current))
  • Other creditors
  • Trade creditors
  • Amounts owed to group undertakings

Notes: Include all amounts which are expected to be paid within 12 months of period end. If the amounts are expected over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end, include only the portion that is due within one year.

Name: Hire purchase: unexpired interest (current)
Detail:
Include
  • Unexpired interest on hire purchase agreements

Notes: Include all unexpired interest due within 12 months of period end. If the amounts are due over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end, include only the portion that is due within one year.

Name: Loans (current)
Detail: Loans can be classified as Secured loans (current), Unsecured loans (current) or Loans from directors (current).

Name: Loans from directors (current)
Detail:
Include
  • Loans from directors (current)

Notes: Include all advance/loans from directors or members of the governing body, whether secured or unsecured, which are expected to be paid within 12 months of period end. If the loans are payable over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end, include only the portion that is expected to be repaid within 12 months of period end (known as the current portion).

Exclude
  • Loans from directors (non-current)

Notes: The non-current portion should be categorised as Loans from directors (non-current) under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities > Loans.

Name: Secured loans (current)
Detail:
Secured loans are loans under which the entity (borrower) has pledged a specific asset or property which may be seized on default of loan obligations.

Include
  • Secured loans (current)
  • Mortgages (current)

Notes: Include the principal portion of secured loans which is expected to be paid within 12 months of period end (known as the current portion). This will often require splitting the current portion and the non-current portion (that is, principal payable beyond 12 months of period end). The non-current portion of secured loans should be categorised as Secured loans (non-current) under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities > Loans.

Exclude
  • Secured loans (non-current)
  • Mortgages (non-current)

Name: Loans from beneficiaries/settlors (current)
Detail:
Include
  • Loans from beneficiaries/settlors (current)

Notes: Include all advance/loans from beneficiaries or settlors, whether secured or unsecured, which are expected to be paid within 12 months of period end. If the loans are payable over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end, include only the portion that is expected to be repaid within 12 months of period end (known as the current portion).

Exclude
  • Loans from beneficiaries/settlors (non-current)

Notes: The non-current portion should be categorised as Loans from beneficiaries/settlors (non-current) under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities > Loans.

Name: Unsecured loans (current)
Detail:
Unsecured loans often have higher interest rates and are supported by personal guarantees.

Include
  • Unsecured loans (current)

Notes: Include the principal portion of unsecured loans which is expected to be paid within 12 months of period end (known as the current portion). This will often require splitting the current portion and the non-current portion (that is, principal payable beyond 12 months of period end).

Exclude
  • Unsecured loans (non-current)
  • Related party receivables
  • Related party payables
  • Related party loans
  • Related party advances

Notes: Do not include loans or advances from related parties. Categorise these as Related party receivables/(payables) under Assets > Current assets. The non-current portion of unsecured loans should be categorised as Unsecured loans (non-current) under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities > Loans.

Name: Trade and other payables
Detail:
Include
  • Credit cards
  • Other payables (creditors)

Notes: Include any other accruals and payables not otherwise separately categorised. These may include credit cards.

Exclude
  • Provisions

Notes: Do not include provisions. Categorise these as Provisions under Liabilities > Current liabilities.

Name: Dividends payable
Detail:
Include
  • Dividends payable on ordinary shares
  • Dividends payable on preference shares
  • Dividends provided for on ordinary shares

Notes: Include all dividends payable at period end.

Include

  • Holiday pay payable
  • Stock options
  • Pension binding obligations

Notes: Include all entitlements owing to employees at period end. These entitlements accumulate to employees as a result of them rendering their services to the entity (employer). They include holiday pay, long service leave, stock options and pension binding obligations.

Name: PAYG
Detail:
Include

  • PAYG refundable

Notes: Include PAYG (Pay As You Go) payable or refundable at period end.

Name: Trade payables
Detail:
Include

Notes: Include only trade payables (also known as trade creditors). These are the amounts owed by the entity for purchases of goods or services.

Exclude

  • Trade payables owed to related parties

Notes: Do not include other payables. Categorise these as Trade and other payables under Liabilities > Current liabilities. Do not include trade payables owed to related parties. Categorise these as Related party receivables/(payables) under Assets > Current assets.

Name: Provisions
Detail:
A provision is a liability uncertain as to timing or amount.

Include

Include all other provisions not otherwise separately categorised.

Name: Related party payables - current
Detail:
Parties are considered related if one party has the ability (directly or indirectly) to control or influence the other party in making operating, investing or financing decisions. Parties are also considered related when they are subject to common outside control or significant influence. Related parties may include subsidiaries, joint ventures, partnerships and associates.

Include
  • Related party payables (current)
  • Advances from related parties (current)
  • Loans from related parties (current)
  • Trade payables to related parties

Notes: Include all advances/loans from related parties which are expected to be paid within 12 months of period end.

Exclude
  • Advances from related parties (non-current)
  • Advances to related parties (current)
  • Advances to related parties (non-current)
  • Beneficiary advance accounts
  • Loans from related parties (non-current)
  • Loans to related parties (current)
  • Loans to related parties (non-current)
  • Partner advance accounts
  • Proprietor advance accounts
  • Related party payables (non-current)
  • Related party receivables (current)
  • Related party receivables (non-current)
  • Settlor loans (if entity is a trust)
  • Shareholder advance accounts
  • Sole trader advance accounts
  • Trade receivables from related parties

Notes: Do not include shareholder, partner, sole trader or beneficiary advance accounts. Categorise these as Proprietor/shareholder advance accounts under Liabilities > Non-current liabilities.

Name: Tax
Detail:
Include

Include all other taxes paid by the entity during the period, or payable by the entity at period end.

Name: Deferred tax
Detail:
A deferred tax asset or liability arises when the carrying value of assets and liabilities differs between what the Inland Revenue has accepted and what the company has reported (that is, different accounting and tax bases). A deferred tax asset may also arise on any unused tax losses of the entity.

Include
  • Deferred tax asset
  • Deferred tax liability

Notes: Include the deferred tax asset or liability at period end.

Name: Goods and services tax
Detail:
Include
  • GST paid
  • GST refunded
  • GST payable
  • GST refundable

Notes: Include Goods and Services Tax (GST) paid during the period, and payable or refundable at period end.

Exclude
  • Penalties
  • Interest charged

Notes: Do not include any penalties and interest charged. Categorise these respectively under Other non-deductible items and Interest and finance charges under Expenses.

Name: Income Tax
Detail: Income tax can be classified as Income tax paid and Income tax refunded.

Name: Franking credits attached to dividends received
Detail:
Include
  • Franking credits on dividends received

Notes: Include Franking Credits attached to dividends received by the entity during the period.

Name: Imputation credits attached to dividends received
Detail:
Include
  • Imputation credits on dividends received

Notes: Include Imputation Credits attached to dividends received by the entity during the period.

Name: Other tax credits
Detail:
Include
  • Include any other tax credits withheld from gross income earned by the entity during the period.
Exclude
  • Withholding tax paid

Name: Income tax paid
Detail:
Include
  • Income tax paid
Exclude
  • Penalties
  • Interest charged

Notes: Do not include any penalties and interest charged. Categorise these respectively under Other non-deductible items and Interest and finance charges under Expenses.

Name: Income tax refunded
Detail:
Include
  • Income tax refunded

Notes: Include income tax refunded during the period in respect of the entity's assessable income.

Name: Tax credits
Detail:
Include
  • Include any other tax credits withheld from gross income earned by the entity during the period.
Exclude
  • Witholding tax paid

Name: Witholding tax paid
Detail:
Withholding Tax is usually deducted on interest and dividend receipts.

Include
  • Witholding tax paid

Notes: Include witholding tax deducted from the gross income earned by the entity during the period.

Name: Loans from directors (non current)
Detail:
Include
  • Loans from directors (non-current)

Notes: Include all advances/loans from directors or members of the governing body, whether secured or unsecured, which are payable over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end.

Exclude
  • Loans from directors (current)

Notes: The principal portion that is expected to be repaid within 12 months of period end (known as the current portion) should be categorised as Loans from directors (current) under Liabilities > Current liabilities > Loans.

Name: Secured loans (non current)
Detail:
Secured loans are loans under which the entity (borrower) has pledged a specific asset or property which may be seized on default of loan obligations.

Include
  • Secured loans (non-current)
  • Mortgages (non-current)

Notes: Include the principal portion of secured loans which is payable beyond 12 months of period end (known as the non-current portion). This will often require splitting the current portion (that is, principal payable within 12 months of period end) and the non-current portion.

Exclude
  • Secured loans (current)
  • Mortgages (current)

Notes: The current portion of secured loans should be categorised as Secured loans (current) under Liabilities > Current liabilities > Loans.

Name: Loans from beneficiaries/settlors (non current)
Detail:
Include
  • Loans from beneficiaries/settlors (non current)

Notes: Include all advances/loans from beneficiaries or settlors, whether secured or unsecured, which are payable over a period extending beyond 12 months of period end.

Exclude

Notes: The principal portion that is expected to be repaid within 12 months of period end (known as the current portion) should be categorised as Loans from directors (current) under Liabilities > Current liabilities > Loans.

Name: Unsecured loans (non current)
Detail:

Include
  • Unsecured loans (non-current)
  • Settlor advances

Notes: Include the principal portion of unsecured loans which is payable beyond 12 months of period end (known as the non-current portion). This will often require splitting the current portion (that is, principal payable within 12 months of period end) and the non-current portion. If the entity is a trust, include settlor advances/loans here.

Exclude
  • Unsecured loans (current)
  • Related party receivables
  • Related party payables
  • Related party loans
  • Related party advances

Notes: The current portion of unsecured loans should be categorised as Unsecured loans (current) under Liabilities > Current liabilities > Loans. Do not include loans or advances from related parties. Categorise these as Related party receivables/(payables) under Assets > Non-current assets.

Exclude

Notes: Do not include depreciation recovered. Categorise this as Gain on disposal of property, plant and equipment under Revenue > Other revenue.

Name: Commissions
Detail:
Include
  • Commissions

Name: Gain on disposal of property, plant & equipment
Detail:
Depreciation recovered arises when the sale price of an asset is higher than that asset's book value. If the sale price is higher than the original cost of that asset, then there will be both depreciation recovered and capital gains.

Include
  • Depreciation recovered on sale of fixed assets

Notes: Include the total amount of depreciation recovered on disposal of fixed assets.

Exclude
  • Capital gain on sale of fixed assets

Notes: Do not include any capital gains made. Categorise these as Capital gain on disposal of asset under Revenue > Other revenue.

Name: Gain on disposal of investments
Detail:
Include
  • Include the gains made on sale of investments (such as subsidiaries, associates and so on).

Name: Trading revenue
Detail:
Trading revenue represents revenue received by the entity from its ordinary activities.

Include

    Notes: Include all other trading revenue not otherwise separately categorised.

    Exclude
    • Other revenue
    • Miscellaneous income

    Notes: Do not include unusual revenue items (miscellaneous income). Categorise this as Other revenue under Revenue.
